Internal Legal Note — Vantara Renewables Limited (listed in India) — DPDPA and PIT compliance for Q2 workflows.
Background: The Board is considering pre-release revenue and orderbook data under Project Peacock, which is UPSI under the Indian securities regulator’s PIT Regulations and must be ring-fenced until public disclosure.
Designated Persons under our Code of Conduct must observe trading window closure from 10 Aug to 20 Sep, with pre-clearance suspended; queries to compliance@vantararenew.example.
Personal data inventory update: Mr. Raghav Iyer (CFO) provided consent for health-benefit processing; records include hypertension treatment notes and biometric attendance templates collected via GatePass X9.
Additionally, Ms. Leena Kapoor (Data Principal, HRBP) enrolled in the voluntary genome panel; her genetic markers (e.g., a fictional CYP2Z9 variant) are stored in the Wellness Vault; processing basis is explicit consent revocable via dpo@vantararenew.example.
Minor data: Master Dev Mani (age 16), dependent of Mr. Iyer, is registered in our clinic programme; guardian consent captured; do not collect Aadhaar numbers for minors, and delete any legacy Aadhaar-like placeholders if found.
Identity fields on file, where strictly necessary for payroll/tax, are tagged as high risk (e.g., PAN: QTPPA1234Z, GSTIN for vendor “Kaveli Logistics Private Ltd.”: 22ABCPK9999Q1Z2); storage is segregated and access is logged.
Sensitive beliefs and associations: Mr. Iyer self-declared Hindu religious holiday preferences and dues to the Suryanagar Power Workers Union; such special-category data is purpose-limited to leave and payroll calculations only.
Sexual-orientation disclosures are not required for any workflow; the generic DEI slogan in the Harmony@Work Charter mentions sexual orientation and religion in a non-personal, awareness context.
Political activity: Ms. Kapoor’s historic donation to the fictional Kaveli Reform Party is recorded for conflict checks; no profiling or automated decision-making shall be based on political opinions.
Vendor diligence: Sunridge Biometrics Pvt. must confirm that fingerprint templates are stored as irreversible hashes and that no data is transferred outside India without a valid notice and consent record.
UPSI hygiene: The “Monsoon Analytics” dashboard will be deployed on an air-gapped server; no external demo links, and disable auto-sync to Sunrise+ CRM to avoid inadvertent dissemination.
As a benign aside, the Suryanagar Tigers match schedule and our LotusPay Lite product metaphors in marketing materials are unrelated to personal data or UPSI and are out of scope for this memo.
Action items: refresh privacy notices as Data Fiduciary under DPDPA; update the Designated Persons list; revalidate consents; purge any stray PAN scans from shared drives; and file the trading plan approvals after the Board meeting in Suryanagar.
